text:
The metal hoard Prosiměřice 2 was uncovered in 2021 during the ongoing archaeological rescue excavation in the Jevišovka River floodplain conducted by Archaeological unit Třebíč since 2006. Due to being buried below the groundwater level, the deposit has not deteriorated and consists of 9 ingots, one casting sprue and two socketed axes that, on the basis of typology, date back to the later period of the Middle Danube Urnfield Culture. The composition of the hoard and its individual objects is however highly varied, containing both pure and impure copper as well as tin bronze. The technical and practical usability of the less than perfect cast axes was confirmed by the combination of use-wear and X-ray computed tomography analyses.

The Prosiměřice 2 hoard is a richer representation of the production chain in one location, similar to the Prosiměřice 1 find. Both were found in the interior of a Linear Pottery open-scattered settlement, and their disposal may also attest to activities of contemporary metalworking at the site. With the disposal to a wetland apparently being deliberate and irreversible, it follows that – similar to other multi-component waterborne caches of the Early Bronze Age – the ritual activity expressed by the disposal of these collections is more related to socio-cultic communities than to practical actions like storage or recovery.
